2606.22486.title: Human and AI collaboration for pulmonary nodule segmentation
2606.22486.paper_summary: Medical expert annotators are scarce, and blind reliance on artificial intelligence (AI) can be misleading, motivating approaches in which humans, particularly junior medical trainees or even non-medical personnel, collaborate with AI to achieve robust medical segmentation. Although the Segment Anything Model (SAM) shows promise for general-purpose image segmentation, its performance in human-AI collaboration for specialized medical tasks has not been thoroughly evaluated. Here we present Hi-Seg, a human-in-the-loop segmentation framework for pulmonary nodules built on SAM. Humans iteratively refine prompts through trial-and-error learning and semantic reasoning, progressively guiding SAM toward higher-quality masks. Using chest CT scans from 1,179 patients across 12 centers, we conducted the first large-scale external validation of collaborative human-SAM segmentation. Across all annotator groups, Hi-Seg achieved a mean Dice score of almost 85%, outperforming five state-of-the-art deep learning models by 10-22% and 13 SAM variants by 1-29%. Hi-Seg improved segmentation accuracy while reducing annotation time for medical annotators, and briefly trained non-medical annotators achieved performance comparable to that of the junior medical student. These findings suggest that human-in-the-loop segmentation can reduce clinician workload, enable scalable crowdsourced annotation, and transform clinical workflows by facilitating the safe and efficient integration of foundation models into routine clinical practice.
2606.22486.paper_summary_zh: 醫療專家標註者稀缺，盲目依賴人工智慧 (AI) 可能會產生誤導，因此促使人類，特別是初級醫療實習生或甚至非醫療人員，與 AI 合作以實現穩健的醫療分割的方法。儘管 Segment Anything Model (SAM) 在通用圖像分割方面顯示出潛力，但其在專業醫療任務中的人類-AI 合作表現尚未得到充分評估。在此，我們提出 Hi-Seg，一個基於 SAM 的肺結節人類參與分割框架。人類通過試錯學習和語義推理反覆精煉提示，逐步引導 SAM 朝向更高質量的標記。使用來自 12 個中心的 1,179 名患者的胸部 CT 掃描，我們進行了首次大規模的外部驗證，評估人類-SAM 協作分割。在所有標註者組中，Hi-Seg 的平均 Dice 分數幾乎達到 85%，超過五個最先進的深度學習模型 10-22% 和 13 個 SAM 變體 1-29%。Hi-Seg 提高了分割準確性，同時減少了醫療標註者的標註時間，而短暫訓練的非醫療標註者的表現達到了與初級醫學生相當的水平。這些發現表明，人類參與的分割可以減輕臨床醫生的工作負擔，實現可擴展的眾包標註，並通過促進基礎模型安全高效地融入日常臨床實踐來改變臨床工作流程。
2606.22486.author: Hongqiao Dong et.al.
2606.22486.authors: Hongqiao Dong, Wenhao Chi, Ruobing Liang, Xiaokui Yang, Wenhua Liang, Peng Hou, Wenjun Pu, Yipeng Zhao, Ping Chen, Haiping Liu, Jianxing He, Bo Liu
